skill.name: theme-handler
skill.description: Apply shadcn UI themes from a curated tweakcn reference list.
skill.summary: Selects tweakcn theme URLs and installs them with the shadcn CLI.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick and weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown code formatting and the word shadcn. The skill still presents moderate risk because it instructs the agent to run pnpm dlx and install remote theme JSON from tweakcn URLs.

medium_findings.title: Remote CLI Theme Installation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add with a selected theme URL. This is a legitimate workflow, but it executes a package from the registry and imports remote content into the project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented and includes remote package execution plus remote theme installation. The behavior appears intentional and useful, but it has supply-chain and project-modification risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Theme URLs
low_findings.description: The reference file contains many hardcoded tweakcn theme URLs. These URLs are expected for the skill, but they create network dependency and require trust in the remote theme source.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in the reference table and example command. They are not hidden or exfiltrating data, so the concern is dependency trust rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick execution alerts are Markdown inline code and fenced code blocks, not Ruby execution. The weak cryptography alert on SKILL.md line 3 has no supporting cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown instructions and a URL table. No Ruby code, crypto API, encoded payload, or executable script file is present in the cited locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command With Remote Input
dangerous_patterns.description: The recommended command uses pnpm dlx to run shadcn and passes a remote theme URL. Users should review theme changes before applying them to a repository.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and remote argument are directly documented. This pattern is common for UI setup, but it can alter project files and fetch external content.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams need a quick way to change visual themes without hunting for valid theme URLs. This skill lists supported tweakcn themes and gives the shadcn command to apply them.
actual_capabilities: Lists available tweakcn theme names and installation URLs.
Provides the shadcn CLI command for a selected theme.
Uses pnpm dlx shadcn@latest for theme installation guidance.
Supports common themes such as Modern Minimal, Vercel, Supabase, and Mono.
Points users to the local reference file for the complete theme list.
limitations: Does not verify that remote theme URLs are still available.
Does not preview visual changes before installation.
Does not validate compatibility with every shadcn project setup.
Requires user approval before running commands that modify project files.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Product UI
use_cases.description: Apply a new shadcn theme to update the look of an existing application quickly.

use_cases.title: Compare Design Directions
use_cases.description: Try several named tweakcn themes during early interface exploration.

use_cases.title: Standardize Demo Styling
use_cases.description: Use a known theme URL to keep demos and prototypes visually consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Named Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this project to use the Modern Minimal theme from the available theme list.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one known theme.

prompt_templates.title: Show Theme Options
prompt_templates.prompt: List five available themes that would fit a clean SaaS dashboard, then recommend one.

prompt_templates.title: Install With Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the Vercel theme, then summarize which files changed and whether the update looks safe.
prompt_templates.scenario: Theme installation with a post-change check.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Multiple Themes
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Supabase, Mono, and Graphite for this app, then apply the best fit after explaining the tradeoffs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced selection based on product context.

output_examples.input: Update the theme to Modern Minimal.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies the Modern Minimal tweakcn URL and prepares the shadcn installation command for that theme.

output_examples.input: Which themes work for a minimal dashboard?
output: Modern Minimal, Vercel, Mono, Clean Slate, and Graphite are suitable options.
The skill can recommend one and provide its installation command.

output_examples.input: Apply the Supabase theme and review the result.
output_examples.output: The skill applies the Supabase theme through shadcn and summarizes the changed project files after installation.

best_practices: Review the selected theme URL before running the install command.
Run the command from the correct project root.
Check the resulting file diff before committing theme changes.
anti_patterns: Do not run the command against an unrelated repository.
Do not install themes from unreviewed URLs outside the reference list.
Do not commit generated theme changes without checking visual and code differences.

faq.question: What does this skill install?
faq.answer: It installs shadcn theme data from tweakcn URLs into a compatible project.

faq.question: Does it create new components?
faq.answer: It focuses on theme installation. Component changes depend on the shadcn command behavior.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow fetches shadcn tooling and theme JSON from external sources.

faq.question: Can I choose a custom theme URL?
faq.answer: The skill is designed around the listed tweakcn URLs. Custom URLs should be reviewed first.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It has moderate supply-chain risk because it runs external tooling, but no malicious intent was found.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
